factors.name: Semiconductor & AI momentum
factors.summary: SK Hynix hit $1T valuation, Micron surged 20.8% today (860% YoY), Samsung workers approved wage deal avoiding strike disruption. Memory chip demand from AI-driven hyperscaler capex remains robust.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk fade (Iran deal)
factors.summary: Unconfirmed Al Jazeera reports suggest US-Iran peace deal 'done,' easing Middle East tension premium that had pressured growth equities. Global stocks extended record rally as risk sentiment improved.

factors.name: China industrial fundamentals
factors.summary: China industrial profits jumped 24.7% YoY in April (fastest in 2+ years), supported by stronger exports and upstream gains. Solid macro backdrop for US tech supply chains and export-sensitive names.

factors.name: RBNZ hawkish hold (casting vote)
factors.summary: RBNZ held OCR at 2.25% but 3-3 split signals coming rate hikes; mixed message on terminal rate (3.2% expected). Tightening bias globally could pressure long-duration tech multiples, but timing uncertainty keeps it neutral.

factors.name: Australia CPI softer (4.2% vs 4.4% expected)
factors.summary: Australian headline CPI eased to 4.2% YoY, undershooting forecasts, but trimmed mean crept to 3.4% (highest since late 2024). Mixed inflation narrative—no clear Fed re-pricing catalyst.

factors.name: Real yields & Fed policy stance
factors.summary: Fed remains on hold; Kashkari notes potential for 'series of hikes' if inflation persists, but no imminent action. Current nominal yields stable; real yields not yet compelling enough to pressure tech multiples significantly.

factors.name: Nasdaq vol (VXN) & options positioning
factors.summary: No explicit VIX/VXN data in news window, but risk-on tone and record rallies suggest complacency. Dealer gamma in mega-caps likely constructive on dips.

factors.name: ETF flows & index inclusion (SpaceX)
factors.summary: FTSE Russell rule change will speed large-cap index inclusion (SpaceX IPO prep), supporting tech/growth ETF inflows. QQQ and XLK momentum intact.

factors.name: Earnings & forward guidance gap
factors.summary: No major NDX mega-cap earnings or guidance in this window; NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings dates unknown from this snapshot. Lack of fresh earnings narrative limits conviction.

factors.name: Dollar weakness (vs yen intervention risk)
factors.summary: USD/JPY lost ground to 159.20 on BoJ intervention speculation; PBOC allowed slight yuan weakness (6.8291 vs estimate 6.7883). Weak dollar could be mild positive for US tech international revenue, but effect is modest.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is riding renewed risk appetite sparked by tentative US-Iran peace deal chatter, strong semiconductor momentum (SK Hynix/Micron/Samsung wage deal avoidance), and robust China industrial profit growth. The geopolitical risk premium is fading, allowing growth/tech to re-rate higher. However, global central banks are signaling future tightening (RBNZ hawkish hold, BoJ warning on persistent oil shocks), which poses a real-yield headwind for long-duration equities if inflation surprises persist. Mega-cap earnings will be the next major catalyst; current move is driven primarily by sentiment and supply-chain stability, not fundamental revaluations.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal official confirmation (within 48h, per Al Jazeera unconfirmed report)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (dates unknown from this window—assumed late May/early June)
PCE inflation data (late May/early June risk)
Fed speakers (Kashkari hawkish comments suggest policy shift watch)
China economic calendar (May industrial production, investment data due)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal fails or tensions reignite, risk-off rotation could unwind the tech rally rapidly. Surprise CPI/PCE upside would trigger Fed re-pricing and real-yield spike, compressing mega-cap valuations. Any reversal in semiconductor demand outlook (hyperscaler capex pullback or inventory builds) would be acutely negative for chip-heavy NDX. Dollar strength vs yen (BoJ intervention ends) could also hurt international revenue perception.
screening.entry_guidance: Early entry on dips to 29900 support; current price near resistance, so wait for pullback before scaling in. Risk-on bias intact but move is fresh (4h +0.35%, 24h +0.02%), so position sizing should reflect that phase-1 catalysts (peace deal) remain unconfirmed.

factors.name: Semiconductor surge
factors.summary: SK Hynix surged 11%+ to cross $1T market cap, Micron also joined $1T club with shares up 20.8% (860% YoY per investinglive wrap). Memory chip frenzy driven by sustained AI demand expectations is lifting NAS100 components directly.

factors.name: Asia tech record highs
factors.summary: Japan Nikkei and South Korea benchmarks hit fresh all-time highs on Wednesday (Reuters, investinglive), with chip-related shares leading. Samsung union wage deal approved (74% support), removing strike risk and supporting global chip supply chain stability.

factors.name: China industrial strength
factors.summary: China industrial profits jumped 24.7% YoY in April (fastest in 2+ years) and YTD profits +18.2% vs prior +15.5%, signaling resilient demand for tech exports and manufacturing despite headwinds. European firms doubling down on China manufacturing per US Top News.

factors.name: Global equity risk-on
factors.summary: Bloomberg: 'Global equities extended their record-breaking rally as momentum in technology shares accelerated and geopolitical tensions eased.' S&P, Nasdaq, Russell 2000 all closed at record levels (investinglive Americas wrap). Tech sentiment broadly positive.

factors.name: Iran tensions easing (partial)
factors.summary: Despite fresh US strikes, markets pricing in progress toward US-Iran peace deal (Bloomberg, WSJ per investinglive). Oil dipped on deal optimism. Al Jazeera unconfirmed reports of deal signing imminent. Reduced geopolitical tail risk supports risk assets.

factors.name: Fed/inflation backdrop
factors.summary: Citadel Securities warns Fed risks falling behind curve, recommends moving toward hikes as inflation becomes dominant risk. Fed's Kashkari mentioned 'series of hikes' in response to inflation (investinglive wrap). BoJ's Ueda warned oil shocks can become persistent. Higher-for-longer rate risk persists.

factors.name: Treasury yields (real rates)
factors.summary: No explicit yield move data in news, but hawkish Fed commentary (Citadel, Kashkari) and persistent inflation concerns (Australia core CPI highest since late 2024, RBNZ hawkish) imply upward pressure on real yields, negative for long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Dollar strength potential
factors.summary: TD Securities holding bearish dollar view for 2026, but acknowledges stronger US data. If Fed turns more hawkish vs peers (RBNZ held dovishly, Australia CPI missed), DXY strength could weigh on NAS100 international revenues. Moderate headwind.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Musk catalysts
factors.summary: SpaceX preparing Nasdaq debut at $1.25T valuation, FTSE rule change speeds index inclusion. Musk discussed SpaceX-Tesla merger (CNBC). SpaceX won $2B Space Force contract. High-profile tech IPO and mega-cap speculation adds froth/momentum to tech narrative.

factors.name: ETF flows & momentum
factors.summary: Record highs across US indices and 'melt-up' talk (MarketWatch: 'S&P 500 to 8,000 or beyond'). HYPE ETF inflows accelerating, crypto-tech correlation positive. Momentum/technical tailwinds intact.

confirmation.narrative: NAS100 is riding a powerful semiconductor-led rally, with SK Hynix and Micron surging into $1T market cap club on AI demand euphoria. Asian tech indices hit record highs and global equity momentum remains intact despite mixed macro signals. The bullish case is clear: chip stocks are core NAS100 constituents, China industrial data surprised to the upside, and geopolitical risk (Iran) is fading. However, the rally is already underway (+0.35% 4h, near ATHs), and hawkish Fed signals (Citadel, Kashkari) plus sticky global inflation (Australia, NZ) create a secondary headwind via higher real yields. This is a Phase 2 scenario—news is bullish, move has started, but not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: Australia April CPI (due ~01:30 GMT Wed) - already released, came in softer than expected at 4.2% vs 4.4% forecast, mild positive
RBNZ policy statement follow-up (hawkish hold, 3-3 split) - NZD volatility, broader risk sentiment watch
US-Iran deal signing rumors (Al Jazeera unconfirmed) - official announcement would be risk-on catalyst
Memorial Day US holiday Mon; watch for low-volume tape into long weekend
Any Fed speaker commentary on inflation/rates (Kashkari hike talk already circulating)
Semiconductor earnings follow-through or guidance updates
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed speakers aggressively frontrun rate hikes, spiking 10Y real yields >2.5%; (2) Iran deal talks collapse and oil surges past $100, reigniting inflation fears; (3) Semiconductor stocks roll over on profit-taking after parabolic moves (Micron +860% YoY is extreme); (4) NAS100 fails to hold 29,800 support, signaling momentum exhaustion. Phase 2 entry risk: chasing here is late—wait for pullback.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is underway and extended short-term. Best risk/reward: wait for pullback to 29,850-29,900 support zone or consolidation break above 30,200 with volume confirmation before adding long exposure.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=BUY vs Sonnet=BULLISH — silent log

factors.name: AI chip momentum (SK Hynix, Samsung, Micron)
factors.summary: SK Hynix hit $1 trillion valuation with 11% intraday surge; Samsung averted strike via wage deal; memory chip 'mania' gripping Asia. AI capex demand narrative remains intact despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: US-Iran ceasefire deal hopes
factors.summary: Al Jazeera reported deal 'done' (unconfirmed); oil slipped below $91 on optimism; geopolitical risk-off sentiment easing. Reduces stagflation shock risk and energy cost pressures on tech.

factors.name: Fed inflation messaging (Kashkari)
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari warned inflation risk now HIGHER than labour market deterioration; cited persistent inflationary shocks from Middle East. Signals less dovish path ahead, pressures long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real yields and 10Y Treasury direction
factors.summary: Bonds rebounded on Iran optimism but hawkish ECB and BoJ rate-hike chatter (RBNZ 3-3 split, 70%+ odds of July hike) keep real yields elevated. Mixed signals—no clear directional conviction.

factors.name: European consumer/growth weakness
factors.summary: French consumer confidence fell to March 2023 lows (82); German growth forecast halved by advisers; Merz cited Middle East and US tariffs as headwinds. Spillover risk for US tech export earnings.

factors.name: China industrial profit strength
factors.summary: China April industrial profits +24.7% y/y (fastest in 2+ years); YTD +18.2%. Energy and export boost supports semiconductor supply chain and eventual demand visibility. Bullish for chip upside.

factors.name: Price action momentum (4h +0.35%, 24h +0.02%)
factors.summary: Shallow gains over short timeframes suggest consolidation or early-stage retest after recent strength. Lacks conviction; move not yet extended.

factors.name: Nasdaq technical structure (30118–30159 resistance zone)
factors.summary: NQ pressing into key resistance after AI IPO frenzy last week; futures remain structurally bullish but 'cooling.' Risk/reward balanced near current levels.

factors.name: Dollar weakness (DXY ~99.10)
factors.summary: USD softening on Iran deal optimism and fading risk-on safe-haven bid. Weaker dollar supports US mega-cap international earnings and helps tech multiples.

factors.name: Earnings and guidance visibility
factors.summary: No major Nasdaq 100 component earnings imminent in news window. Hyperscaler capex outlook remains strong on AI, but no fresh catalysts cited.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in a shallow rally recovery supported by persistent AI demand (SK Hynix, Samsung, memory chips) and easing geopolitical risk (Iran deal optimism, oil down). However, Fed hawkishness on inflation (Kashkari messaging) and European growth/consumer weakness temper upside enthusiasm. The move is early-stage and lacks conviction; price action is consolidating near key technical resistance, with real yields still elevated and rate-hike odds rising globally. This is a transition from risk-off to risk-on, but with unresolved macro headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran framework agreement/MOU signing (potentially imminent—hours to 1-2 days)
Major tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL, META, TSLA) — dates TBD, typically late May into early June
US jobs report / NFP (early June typical)
Hyperscaler cloud capex guidance updates (earnings season)
China policy response or data surprises (tariff, credit, growth)
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation: If Iran deal collapses or escalates further, oil spikes back above $95–100, stagflation fears resume, and real yields spike—this would flip bias to SELL and trigger 2–3% NAS100 drawdown. Alternatively, if Fed pivots dovish (inflation surprise lower), resistance break and 7–8 confidence BUY is warranted; conversely, if Kashkari's hawkish messaging spreads through FOMC and rate-hike odds climb further, 10Y yields re-test highs and long-duration tech gets hit.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (early but not extended). On retest of 29950–30000 support, consider entry; resistance at 30118–30159 is firm—wait for clean break above with volume to chase. Do NOT chase into resistance; prefer pullback entry as deal-headline volatility will likely create 0.3–0.5% swings.
